title: Create API request/response models with Schematic derivation
Add BoogieLoops schema + web dependencies to build.mill
Add Schematic derivation to StoredObject model
Update tests for new route signatures
Migrate ObjectStorage to BoogieLoops schema + web
Verify OpenAPI generation and delete manual openapi.yaml
Migrate FileRoutes to Web.* annotations (all endpoints)
Fix NDJSON append returning HTTP 400 despite successful write
description:
## Problem

The `POST /api/v1/files/:objectId/ndjson/append/stream` endpoint returns HTTP 400 with error message `"Failed to append stream content: UT000034: Stream is closed"` even though the data is successfully written to disk.

## Root Cause

In `FileManager.appendToFileStreamLocked()`, the `geny.Readable` request stream is consumed and written to a temp file, then appended to the target file. After the operation completes successfully, Undertow closes the exchange stream. The response is sent after/during this close, causing Undertow to report `UT000034: Stream is closed` as an error — even though the append operation already completed.

## Impact

- Clients must ignore the 400 status and treat it as success, which is fragile
- The response body says `"status": "error"` when the operation actually succeeded
- Callers cannot distinguish between a real failure and this false error

## Expected Behavior

Append should return HTTP 200 with `"status": "success"` when data is written.

## Reproduction

```bash
# Create a file
OID=$(curl -s -X POST "https://s3.audetic.link/api/v1/files" \
  -H "x-tenant-id: test" -H "x-user-id: test" -H "x-api-key: <key>" \
  -H "x-file-name: test.ndjson" -H "x-mimetype: application/x-ndjson" \
  --data-binary $'{"init":true}\n' | grep -o '"objectId":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)

# Append — returns 400 but data IS written
curl -s -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}" -X POST \
  "https://s3.audetic.link/api/v1/files/$OID/ndjson/append/stream" \
  -H "x-tenant-id: test" -H "x-user-id: test" -H "x-api-key: <key>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-ndjson" \
  --data-binary $'{"e":"test"}\n'
# Returns: {"message":"Failed to append stream content: UT000034: Stream is closed","status":"error"}
# HTTP 400

# But read back shows data was written:
curl -s "https://s3.audetic.link/api/v1/files/$OID/ndjson/items/stream" \
  -H "x-tenant-id: test" -H "x-user-id: test" -H "x-api-key: <key>"
# Returns: {"init":true}  {"e":"test"}
```

## Relevant Code

- `ObjectStorage/src/objectstorage/routes/FileRoutes.scala` lines 347-397 (append route)
- `ObjectStorage/src/objectstorage/filemanager/FileManager.scala` `appendToFileStreamLocked()`

## Additional Note

NDJSON lines must be `\n`-terminated by the caller — otherwise consecutive appends concatenate on the same line. This should be documented in the API docs.
